title: Abort: pending-exception assert from `warn_explicit` normalization (`_warnings.c:799/806`)
description: When a warning is emitted while an allocation failure is active, the warnings C path (do_warn -> warn_explicit) reaches message normalization with a MemoryError already pending. warn_explicit then normalizes the message with an exception set: PyObject_CallOneArg(category, message) -> type_call (typeobject.c:2441) for a plain-string message, or PyObject_Str(message) (object.c:818) for a Warning-instance message. Both trip the debug-only invariant assert `!_PyErr_Occurred(tstate)` -> SIGABRT on debug builds. The assert is #ifdef Py_DEBUG, so release builds compile it out and silently lose the exception (latent invariant violation).
notes: Debug-only assert: warn_explicit normalizes the warning message with a pending exception under OOM. Two manifestations of one bug, adjacent lines in warn_explicit: plain-string message -> _warnings.c:806 -> type_call (typeobject.c:2441) [fleet vehicle]; Warning-instance message -> _warnings.c:799 -> PyObject_Str (object.c:818) [minimal repro, deterministic 3/3]. The MemoryError is already pending on entry to warn_explicit (gdb: PyErr at the crashing invocation), so the missing error check is in do_warn/warn_explicit (or the caller emitting the warning) before message normalization. Stale-exception-under-OOM family (cf. OOM-0008/0010/0011/0015), distinct site. NOT the same as OOM-0001 (do_warn:1139 Py_DECREF-of-freed segv) although the same fleet vehicle triggers OOM-0032 on the debug build and OOM-0001 on release builds (classic one-vehicle-multiple-bugs / build-dependent face). Minimal repro hits the PyObject_Str sibling; the exact type_call:2441 window is vehicle-confirmed (a plain-string minimal sweep segfaults in the warnings path before reaching it). Found by the LOCAL systemd fleet, flagged oomNEW, surfaced by the native-backtrace ingest sweep over the 31-bug catalog.
